taylor-williams.female_specific_note: Taylor Williams Coaching focuses on female-specific HRT/peptide guidance. Per-peptide notes here often emphasize female dosing, fertility, hormone interactions.

reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the practitioner to consider Semax as a neurotrophic / cognitive-support peptide with the strongest expert overlap on dopamine + serotonin modulation, BDNF/TrkB signaling, and a Russian clinical track record cited as remarkably safe — Trevor (X4NsP-yhD-8 00:27:57.760): "Decades of clinical use in Russia for stroke, brain injury, vascular disorders. Zero. I'll say it again. Zero adverse events. Impeccable toxicological profile." We'd ask the prescribing physician to weigh route carefully because the experts diverge sharply: Taylor (2m6fKa4Wd6k 00:07:14.520) "for me I prefer nasal spray" while Trevor (X4NsP-yhD-8 00:18:33.280) says "stop squirting it in your nose. By the way, nasal sprays are bunk." Hunter (email threadId 19c06d9325e3862f 2026-01-28) frames Semax as gender-neutral: "Semax + Selank — neurotrophic signaling (BDNF/TrkB) + immune/cytokine modulation. Don't require gender dosing."
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- Route dispute (nasal vs subq): Taylor (2m6fKa4Wd6k 00:06:16.360) "inter nasally so with a nasal spray applicator" and (00:06:29.960) "when you do it inner nasal spray is that it's going directly to the brain quicker"; Trevor (X4NsP-yhD-8 00:18:33.280) "stop squirting it in your nose. By the way, nasal sprays are bunk" and (00:18:43.120) "the nasal membranes are not designed to transport in. It's not how it works."
- Cycling required: Trevor (X4NsP-yhD-8 00:24:16.480) "four weeks on, two weeks off" and (00:24:23.840) "you just want to prevent receptor down regulation, maintain efficacy."
- Foundation-first sequencing: Hunter email threadId 195b0718ac68bbb2 (2025-03-19) "no amount of selank, semax, or cerebrolysin will fully optimize your brain health. Start journaling consistently, THEN invest in peptides."
- Specific clinical indications cited: Hunter email threadId 19d98523208598ae (2026-04-16) "Semax — for cerebral ischemia, migraine, trigeminal neuralgia."
- Regulatory status (April 2026): Hunter email threadId 19d98523208598ae (2026-04-16) lists Semax among 12 peptides removed from FDA Category 2; PCAC review July 24, 2026 — clinical-pathway access changing.
- Hunter does NOT publish a Semax-specific dose ceiling in any of the surfaced emails — he only frames it as unisex receptor-binding neurotrophic peptide. Trevor does not state a milligram dose in the surfaced episode either; only cycling and route guidance.
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus: Mechanism — neurotrophic / BDNF + cognitive-support: Hunter email threadId 19c06d9325e3862f (2026-01-28): "Semax + Selank — neurotrophic signaling (BDNF/TrkB) + immune/cytokine modulation." Taylor (2m6fKa4Wd6k 00:02:46.200): "neuroprotective effects as well" and (2m6fKa4Wd6k 00:02:54.879): "it is going to help so much with your dopamine and serotonin levels." Trevor (X4NsP-yhD-8 00:25:08.000): describes a stack where "the army is cerebral. This is a a peptide cocktail that is a direct infusion that just soaks you neurotrphic factors." Cognitive use case: Hunter email threadId 19829deefaf29db8 (2025-07-20): "peptides known to boost cognitive function (think Dihexa, Semax, Cerebrolysin), you get a synergistic effect which leads to more neural growth, better mood, and faster recovery from mental fatigue." Hunter email threadId 19c112a2709941b7 (2026-01-30): "Cerebrolysin alternatives: P21, PE-22-28, Selank, Semax." Taylor (2m6fKa4Wd6k 00:02:08.000): "is a peptide that I have recently been using and it has made a huge difference in my brain cognition." Unisex dosing: Hunter email threadId 19c06d9325e3862f (2026-01-28): "Don't require gender dosing."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement: Route disagreement is the central conflict. Taylor (2m6fKa4Wd6k 00:06:16.360): "inter nasally so with a nasal spray applicator and you can use this um as a sub cute injection and I have done that and it works really great" and Taylor (2m6fKa4Wd6k 00:07:14.520): "for me I prefer nasal spray." Taylor (2m6fKa4Wd6k 00:06:29.960): "when you do it inner nasal spray is that it's going directly to the brain quicker." Trevor (X4NsP-yhD-8 00:18:33.280): "stop squirting it in your nose. By the way, nasal sprays are bunk." Trevor (X4NsP-yhD-8 00:18:43.120): "the nasal membranes are not designed to transport in. It's not how it works." Dose: only Taylor publishes a number — Taylor (2m6fKa4Wd6k 00:06:09.800): "I like to take anywhere between 200 to 600 micrograms." Hunter does not publish a Semax-specific dose in the surfaced emails (his email-summary 500 mcg / 20 mg / 120 mg numbers belong to a Pinealon protocol that merely lists Semax as a stack partner, not as Semax dosing). Trevor does not publish a numeric Semax dose in the surfaced episode. Frequency: Taylor (2m6fKa4Wd6k 00:07:21.639): "you can actually take this anywhere between two to three times a day" and (2m6fKa4Wd6k 00:07:35.759): "I like to use it once a day." Cycling: Trevor (X4NsP-yhD-8 00:24:16.480): "four weeks on, two weeks off" — Taylor's 2024-11-15 video does not state a Semax-specific cycling window in the surfaced passages.

chronic.dose: Taylor (2m6fKa4Wd6k 00:06:09.800): "I like to take anywhere between 200 to 600 micrograms" — only expert publishing a numeric Semax dose in surfaced sources. Hunter and Trevor do not state a Semax-specific milligram/microgram dose in the surfaced sources.
chronic.route: Disputed: Taylor (2m6fKa4Wd6k 00:07:14.520): "for me I prefer nasal spray" and (2m6fKa4Wd6k 00:06:22.599): "you can use this um as a sub cute injection and I have done that and it works really great." Trevor (X4NsP-yhD-8 00:18:33.280): "stop squirting it in your nose. By the way, nasal sprays are bunk" and (X4NsP-yhD-8 00:18:43.120): "the nasal membranes are not designed to transport in."
chronic.frequency: Taylor (2m6fKa4Wd6k 00:07:21.639): "you can actually take this anywhere between two to three times a day"; Taylor (2m6fKa4Wd6k 00:07:35.759): "I like to use it once a day."
chronic.duration: Trevor (X4NsP-yhD-8 00:24:16.480): "four weeks on, two weeks off"; Trevor (X4NsP-yhD-8 00:24:23.840): "you just want to prevent receptor down regulation, maintain efficacy."
chronic.verbatim_citation: Taylor (2m6fKa4Wd6k 00:06:09.800): "I like to take anywhere between 200 to 600 micrograms." Trevor (X4NsP-yhD-8 00:24:16.480): "four weeks on, two weeks off."
chronic.timestamp: Taylor 00:06:09.800 dose; Taylor 00:07:14.520 route; Trevor X4NsP-yhD-8 00:24:16.480 cycling; Trevor X4NsP-yhD-8 00:18:33.280 nasal-bunk counterpoint

reset_biology_synthesis.female_specific_notes: Taylor's 2024-11-15 Semax-vs-Selank video frames Semax as the focus/cognition tool and reserves Selank for hormone-cycle-driven anxiety: Taylor (2m6fKa4Wd6k 00:17:29.200): "my lit phase of my menstrual cycles are tough" and (2m6fKa4Wd6k 00:17:33.039): "I definitely will use slink during my Ludo phase of my cycle especially if I'm having to be social." No female-specific Semax dose ceiling is stated; Taylor uses the same 200-600 mcg range she gives generally. Hunter's gender-dosing email is explicit that Semax does NOT require sex-specific adjustment — Hunter email threadId 19c06d9325e3862f (2026-01-28): "Semax + Selank — neurotrophic signaling (BDNF/TrkB) + immune/cytokine modulation. Don't require gender dosing."
